content.text:
NDA Review Playbook â€” In-House Commercial Counsel

This playbook governs first-pass review of any inbound Non-Disclosure / Confidentiality Agreement (NDA) where our business is being asked to receive Confidential Information, disclose Confidential Information, or both. It applies regardless of counterparty type, deal context, or originating jurisdiction.

# 1. Triage rubric â€” GREEN / YELLOW / RED

Every NDA gets a single-letter verdict. Reviewers should be able to triage a typical 2-3 page NDA in 5-10 minutes using this rubric.

- **GREEN** â€” balanced mutual or bilateral NDA with standard carve-outs (publicly known, prior knowledge, third-party receipt, independent development, compelled disclosure), reasonable term (1-3 years operative + 1-5 years survival), our preferred or a neutral governing law, no unusual restrictions. Can go straight to signature without legal review.
- **YELLOW** â€” material but negotiable issue: long survival (more than 7 years), broad CI definition (catches publicly-available information, or any information disclosed in any context), one-sided obligations, non-solicitation clauses, no-publicity clauses, unfavourable governing law but otherwise normal. Needs legal review and one round of redlining; usually closeable.
- **RED** â€” at least one of the following: perpetual / indefinite term, no carve-outs at all, extraterritorial jurisdiction we don't operate in (and which would expose us to costly forum-shopping), liquidated-damages or per-incident penalty clauses, non-compete clauses dressed up as NDA terms, IP assignment language smuggled into the confidentiality terms, no-residuals carve-out. Needs proper legal involvement; not signed without partner-level decision.

# 2. The flag list â€” what to look for

Read every NDA looking for these. Each is a routine triage trigger.

## 2.1 Term and survival

- **Indefinite / perpetual obligations** â†’ RED. No business case justifies giving an unlimited-duration confidentiality undertaking; it surfaces 20 years later when no one remembers why and creates discovery exposure.
- **Survival of more than 7 years** â†’ YELLOW. Push to 3 years from disclosure unless there's a good reason (e.g., long-running regulatory matter, trade-secret content).
- **Operative term of more than 5 years** â†’ YELLOW. Most discussions are over within 12-18 months.

## 2.2 Definition of Confidential Information

- **Captures publicly available information** â†’ YELLOW or RED depending on phrasing. "Information that is publicly available but compiled or contextualised by the disclosing party" is YELLOW; "any information disclosed in any context" is RED.
- **Excludes residuals carve-out** â†’ YELLOW. Sophisticated NDAs allow residuals retained in unaided memory of personnel; absence is a flag.
- **Captures derivative works** â†’ YELLOW. Standard wording covers the information itself, not derivative analysis the receiving party generates from public sources informed by the discussions.

## 2.3 Carve-outs

- **Missing standard carve-outs** (publicly known, prior knowledge, third-party receipt, independent development, compelled disclosure) â†’ YELLOW or RED.
- **Single-carve-out clauses** ("only information already in our written records prior to disclosure") â†’ RED.
- **Compelled-disclosure carve-out without prompt-notice obligation** â†’ YELLOW. Buyer wants notice so they can seek a protective order; absence is a flag.

## 2.4 Governing law and jurisdiction

- **Governing law from a jurisdiction the receiving party doesn't operate in** â†’ YELLOW. Push for our local law or a neutral choice (English law is widely acceptable).
- **Exclusive jurisdiction in counterparty's home court** â†’ YELLOW. Cost-of-litigation flag.
- **Extraterritorial reach** ("subject to the laws of [Texas / California / Singapore] without regard to conflict-of-laws principles") â†’ YELLOW or RED. Conflict-of-laws principles waiver is sometimes a smuggled forum-shopping play.

## 2.5 Beyond-confidentiality clauses

These are often the real risk â€” clauses that aren't about confidentiality at all but get smuggled in under an NDA cover.

- **Non-compete** â†’ RED. Confidentiality and non-compete are different agreements; combining them is usually a sign of buyer trying to lock the seller out of a market via an NDA negotiating window.
- **Non-solicitation** â†’ YELLOW if narrow (employees actually in contact, 12 months); RED if broad (any employee, any indirect approach, 24+ months).
- **IP assignment** â†’ RED. NDAs are not the place for IP transfer. If the counterparty wants assignment, that's a separate paid licence or development agreement.
- **Liquidated damages** â†’ RED. The party drafting an NDA with a $500k-per-incident clause has thought hard about how to extract value from an alleged breach; we shouldn't sign without serious legal involvement.
- **No-publicity clauses extending beyond announcing the deal** â†’ YELLOW. Standard "no public statements about the discussions" is fine; "no statements about the counterparty for 2 years post-termination" is YELLOW.
- **Reverse-engineering restrictions** in a confidentiality clause â†’ YELLOW or RED depending on context. Make sense for software; not for general commercial NDAs.

## 2.6 Mutual vs bilateral

- **Mutual** NDAs apply confidentiality obligations to both parties. Preferred default for any two-way commercial discussion.
- **Bilateral / one-way** NDAs apply obligations only to the receiving party. Acceptable when we are clearly receiving Confidential Information for a defined evaluation purpose (e.g., evaluating a vendor's pricing methodology). Not acceptable when there is genuine two-way disclosure.
- **One-way running TO us as discloser** â†’ GREEN typically.
- **One-way running FROM us as receiver** without a clear scope â†’ YELLOW.

# 3. Output format

For batch review (multiple NDAs), produce a single table:

| # | Counterparty | Verdict | Top Issues | Action |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | [Counterparty A] | GREEN | none | Sign |
| 2 | [Counterparty B] | YELLOW | Long survival, no residuals | Push to 3+3, add residuals, then sign |
| 3 | [Counterparty C] | RED | Perpetual, no carve-outs, $500k LDs | Escalate to GC; not signable as drafted |

For per-NDA detail, follow with one paragraph each on the RED and YELLOW items, naming the clause and the proposed redline shape.

# 4. Sign-off rules

- **GREEN** â€” counsel sign or commercial sponsor sign with no legal involvement required.
- **YELLOW** â€” counsel reviews and signs after redline; flag survival/CI-definition changes to the relationship lead.
- **RED** â€” General Counsel must approve. Senior commercial sponsor must sign.

# 5. Counterparty correspondence

When responding to a RED NDA with substantive issues, write to the counterparty's legal team (not their commercial team â€” they will not have authority to amend their standard NDA). Use this shape:

1. Thanks for the NDA.
2. Identify the 2-3 issues we cannot accept (do not enumerate every YELLOW item â€” pick the ones that genuinely block).
3. For each, name the clause and propose specific alternative wording.
4. Offer to discuss; do not propose multiple alternatives in the first message (creates the impression of unlimited negotiating room).

# 6. When this playbook does not apply

- Settlement NDAs (different shape; usually negotiated with disputes counsel).
- M&A confidentiality agreements (typically NDA + standstill + exclusivity; specialist M&A counsel).
- Government / classified-information NDAs (specialist regulatory counsel).
- Employment-related confidentiality undertakings (HR counsel).

This playbook covers commercial-counterparty NDAs. Anything else, escalate.

content.text:
# Loaded Skill: nda-review (skill)

## nda-review (skill)

Reference: fast triage of inbound NDAs into GREEN / YELLOW / RED so the team only spends lawyer time on the ones that need it. Built for sales and BD to self-serve before pinging legal. Loaded by review when an NDA is detected.


### Content

<!-- Sourced from anthropics/claude-for-legal/commercial-legal @ 4d55f539; Apache 2.0 -->
<!-- Oscar GC modifications (Sprint 12, ADR-037): matter paths rewritten to $OSCAR_MATTER_DIR -->

# NDA Review

## Matter context

**Matter context.** Check `## Matter workspaces` in the practice-level CLAUDE.md. If `Enabled` is `âś—` (the default for in-house users), skip the rest of this paragraph â€” skills use practice-level context and the matter machinery is invisible. If enabled and there is no active matter, ask: "Which matter is this for? Run `matter-workspace switch <slug>` or say `practice-level`." Load the active matter's `matter.md` for matter-specific context and overrides. Write outputs to the matter folder at `$OSCAR_MATTER_DIR/`. Never read another matter's files unless `Cross-matter context` is `on`.

---

## Destination check

Before producing output, check where it's going. If the user has named a destination (a channel, a distribution list, a counterparty, "everyone"), ask whether it's inside the privilege circle. Public channels, company-wide lists, counterparty/opposing counsel, vendors, and clients (for work product) waive the protection. When the destination looks outside the circle, flag it and offer (a) the privileged version for legal only, (b) a sanitized version for the broader channel, or (c) both â€” don't silently apply a privileged header and then help paste it somewhere the header won't protect it. See the canonical `## Shared guardrails â†’ Destination check` in this plugin's CLAUDE.md.

## Purpose

Most inbound NDAs are fine. A few have landmines. This skill sorts them in under a minute so legal only reads the ones that matter.

**The goal:** a GREEN NDA should need nothing more than a signature. A YELLOW needs a lawyer's eyes on one or two specific things. A RED stops before anyone wastes time.

## Load the playbook first

**Which side?** Before applying the playbook, determine which side the company is on for this NDA. Usually obvious from the context: if the counterparty is a vendor or partner evaluating your product, you're sales-side; if you're evaluating theirs, you're purchasing-side. Mutual NDAs still have a side â€” whose paper is it, and which direction is the evaluation running. If it's not obvious, ask. Read the matching playbook section (`### Sales-side playbook` or `### Purchasing-side playbook`) from the config. Note which side in the output so the reviewer knows which playbook was applied. If the matching side is `[Not configured]`, stop and tell the user to run `Oscar GC onboarding --side <side>` before this triage can proceed.

**Before triaging anything, read `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` â†’ `## Playbook` â†’ the matching side â†’ `NDA triage positions`.** That section is the source of truth for what makes an NDA GREEN, YELLOW, or RED for *this* team on *this* side. This skill does not ship with default positions on NDA terms â€” the law, the market, and each team's risk tolerance vary too much for hardcoded defaults to be safe.

If `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` doesn't have an `NDA triage positions` section yet, or it's silent on a term that comes up in the NDA you're reviewing, ask the user:

> Your playbook doesn't cover [term â€” e.g., "residuals clauses," "survival period," "one-way NDAs where you're the receiver"]. What's your default position â€” when should this be GREEN, when YELLOW, when RED? I'll add it to `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` so the next review is consistent.

Then record the answer in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` and proceed with the triage using the new position.

## Scope check

**Before reviewing NDA-specific provisions, check whether the document is doing more than its name suggests.** Mutual commercial NDAs can hide: standstills, licensing grants, exclusivity, non-solicits, non-competes, IP assignments, right of first refusal, most-favored-nation clauses, and arbitration/jurisdiction clauses that govern far more than confidentiality disputes.

If the NDA contains obligations beyond confidentiality: **auto-YELLOW regardless of the NDA-term analysis.** Flag the non-NDA provisions:

> This document is labeled an NDA but contains [standstill / license grant / non-solicit / exclusivity / IP assignment / ROFR / MFN / broad arbitration]. It's more than an NDA. Route for attorney review.

Do not silently push a document labeled "NDA" through NDA triage when the substantive obligations are a services agreement, a term sheet, or a covenant package in NDA clothing.

## The triage

Classify the NDA into one of three buckets by applying the positions from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. The bucket definitions below are stable; the *criteria* that fill each bucket come from the playbook.

### GREEN â€” route to signature

The NDA satisfies every position in the team's playbook, and no term triggers a RED flag per the playbook. Examples of checks the playbook typically covers: mutuality, term length, survival period, carveouts, governing law, restrictive covenants, fee-shifting. Confirm each one against `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` before calling GREEN.

**GREEN requires attorney-reviewed playbook positions.** GREEN is the only path to signature without lawyer review. It cannot be issued against default or absent positions. Before issuing GREEN, check: does the practice profile have an attorney-reviewed `## NDA triage positions` section? If not:

> I can't issue GREEN without attorney-reviewed NDA positions in your practice profile. Run `Oscar GC onboarding --full` with your commercial counsel to set them, or route this NDA for attorney review. Issuing GREEN against defaults means a non-lawyer set the positions the next non-lawyer relies on.

Do not route to signature on defaults. YELLOW is the right call when positions are missing â€” it surfaces the NDA to a human who can decide.

**Output:**

Prepend the work-product header from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` `## Outputs` (it differs by user role â€” see `## Who's using this`).

```markdown
[WORK-PRODUCT HEADER â€” per plugin config ## Outputs]

## NDA Triage: [Counterparty]

GREEN â€” route to signature

### Executive Summary

No red flags identified under the playbook. Route for signature per standard process.

| Check | Status | Playbook reference |
|---|---|---|
| [Each playbook check] | [pass/fail] | [`~/.config/oscar/profile.json` section] |

**Next step:** [Submit to [CLM] standard NDA workflow | Send to [approver from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`] for signature]
```

**Before proceeding past GREEN to signature:** Read `## Who's using this` in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the Role is Non-lawyer:

> This step has legal consequences (countersigning an NDA binds the company). Have you reviewed this with an attorney? If yes, proceed. If no, here's a brief to bring to them:
>
> [Generate a 1-page summary: counterparty, NDA direction (mutual / one-way), the playbook checks run, anything the playbook didn't cover, what could go wrong if signed as-is, and the three things to ask the attorney.]
>
> If you need to find an attorney, solicitor, barrister, or other authorised legal professional: contact your professional regulator (state bar in the US, SRA/Bar Standards Board in England & Wales, Law Society in Scotland/NI/Ireland/Canada/Australia, or your jurisdiction's equivalent) for a referral service.

Do not proceed past this gate without an explicit yes.

### YELLOW â€” needs a lawyer's eyes on specific items

One or more terms deviate from the playbook but aren't categorical deal-breakers, OR a term appears that the playbook doesn't address. Surface each item individually so the approver can make the call.

**Output:**

Prepend the work-product header from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` `## Outputs` (it differs by user role â€” see `## Who's using this`).

```markdown
[WORK-PRODUCT HEADER â€” per plugin config ## Outputs]

## NDA Triage: [Counterparty]

YELLOW â€” flag for [approver name from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`]

### Executive Summary

- [One-line actionable edit, e.g. "Strike non-solicit clause (Section 6)"]
- [One-line actionable edit]

### Flagged items

**1. [Issue]** â€” Section [X]
   What: [one line]
   Why flagged: [one line â€” which playbook position this hits, or "playbook is silent on this"]
   **Legal risk:** [đź”´/đźź /đźźˇ/đźź˘] | **Business friction:** [đź”´ Blocks deals / đźź  Slows deals / đźźˇ Confuses customers / đźź˘ Invisible]
   Likely resolution: [accept / push back on X / depends on deal context]

[repeat for each flag]

### Everything else

| Check | Status | Playbook reference |
|---|---|---|
| [playbook checks that passed] | pass | [`~/.config/oscar/profile.json` section] |

**Next step:** Ask [approver] about the flagged items, then route to signature if they're okay with it.
```

### RED â€” stop, talk to legal first

The NDA hits a position on the playbook's "never accept" list, or the structure of the agreement is incompatible with the team's standard posture (e.g., a one-way NDA where the team's playbook requires mutual; a perpetual term where the playbook caps at a finite period; governing law on the "never" list).

**Output:**

Prepend the work-product header from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` `## Outputs` (it differs by user role â€” see `## Who's using this`).

```markdown
[WORK-PRODUCT HEADER â€” per plugin config ## Outputs]

## NDA Triage: [Counterparty]

RED â€” do not submit, talk to legal first

### Executive Summary

- [One-line actionable edit, e.g. "Section 4 â€” route to Legal for review"]
- [One-line actionable edit]

### Critical issues

**1. [Issue]** â€” Section [X]
   > "[exact quote]"
   Why this is a problem: [specific risk; cite the playbook position it violates]
   **Legal risk:** [đź”´/đźź /đźźˇ/đźź˘] | **Business friction:** [đź”´ Blocks deals / đźź  Slows deals / đźźˇ Confuses customers / đźź˘ Invisible]
   Recommended response: [use our paper instead | push back with specific language | walk]

**Next step:** Send this triage to [GC or named escalation person from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`]. Do not send to [CLM or approvals workflow]. Do not tell the counterparty we'll sign.
```

## Redline granularity

**Edit at the smallest possible granularity.** A redline is a negotiation artifact, not a rewrite. Wholesale clause replacement signals "we threw out your drafting" â€” it's aggressive, it forces the counterparty to re-read the whole clause, and it discards the parts of their drafting that were fine. Surgical redlines â€” strike a word, insert a phrase, restructure a subclause â€” signal "we have specific asks" and are faster to read, understand, and accept.

Default to the smallest edit that achieves the playbook position:
- Replace a **word** before a phrase. ("twelve (12)" â†’ "twenty-four (24)")
- Replace a **phrase** before a sentence. ("paid by the Buyer" â†’ "paid and payable by the Buyer")
- Restructure a **subclause** before replacing the sentence. (Add "(a)" and "(b)" to split a compound condition.)
- Replace a **sentence** before replacing the clause.
- Only replace a **whole clause** when the counterparty's version is so far from your position that surgical edits would be harder to read than a fresh draft â€” and when you do, say so in the transmittal: "We've replaced Â§8.2 rather than marking it up because the changes were extensive. Happy to walk you through the delta."

When in doubt, smaller. A client who receives a surgical redline trusts that you read carefully. A client who receives a wholesale replacement wonders whether you read at all.

## Jurisdiction assumption

This triage applies the governing-law and restrictive-covenant positions recorded in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. Legal rules (enforceability of non-competes, non-solicits, fee-shifting, choice of law) vary materially by jurisdiction. If the NDA involves a jurisdiction outside the team's configured posture, flag it in the output and note that the triage may not transfer as written.

## Output rules

**Complexity filter:** If addressing an issue would require drafting new
language, restructuring a clause, or inserting substantive new
provisions â€” do not attempt it. Instead write:
"Section [X] â€” route to Legal for review."
Only include simple, mechanical actions in the Executive Summary
(strike, delete, replace a word or phrase).

**Clean NDA rule:** If the NDA passes all checks with no flags, the Executive Summary
should say only: "No red flags identified. Route for signature per
standard process."

Do not produce a lengthy report for a clean NDA.

## Detailed check reference

For each check below, the bucket (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) is determined by `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. This skill lists the *categories* to check; it does not hardcode thresholds.

### Mutuality

Is the NDA mutual or one-way? Apply the team's position from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the playbook doesn't address one-way NDAs for this context, run the one-way questionnaire below and surface the result for a human.

**One-way NDA questionnaire**

When the NDA is unilateral (one party discloses, the other only receives), do not immediately flag RED or exit. Ask:

> A one-way NDA is appropriate in some situations. Before flagging this,
> let me ask a few quick questions:
>
> 1. In this relationship, are you the only party disclosing confidential
>    information? (i.e., the other side shares nothing back)
> 2. Is this for a limited, specific disclosure â€” for example, sharing
>    your technology with a vendor who will work on it, but not sharing
>    theirs with you?
> 3. Is this related to M&A, employment, or investment? (If yes, stop â€”
>    this skill is for commercial MNDAs only. Route to Legal.)

Use the answers plus the `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` position to decide GREEN/YELLOW/RED. If `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` doesn't take a position on this fact pattern, flag YELLOW and surface the questionnaire answers for the approver.

### Definition of Confidential Information

Check scope (marked-only vs. everything-disclosed), marking requirements, and oral-disclosure confirmation windows. Apply the team's position from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the playbook is silent on any of these, ask.

### Carveouts

The five carveouts typically present in an NDA:

1. Information that is or becomes public (other than through breach)
2. Information the receiving party already had
3. Information independently developed without reference to the CI
4. Information received from a third party without restriction
5. Information required to be disclosed by law or court order (with notice to discloser where legally permitted)

Which carveouts the team requires, and how strictly, is a playbook question. Check `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` for the team's position on required carveouts, acceptable variations in wording, and what happens when one is missing.

### Residuals

A residuals clause lets the receiving party use information retained in unaided memory. Whether this is acceptable â€” and under what conditions (e.g., narrow "unaided memory" wording vs. broader scope covering notes or copies) â€” is a playbook question. Apply `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the playbook doesn't address residuals, ask.

### Term and survival

Check the initial term length, the post-term survival period for confidentiality obligations, and whether trade secrets are carved out with longer protection. Apply the team's position from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the playbook doesn't cover one of these, ask.

### Restrictive covenants

Check for non-solicits (employee, customer), non-competes, exclusivity, and any restriction on who else the receiving party can engage with. Apply `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`. If the playbook is silent, ask â€” restrictive covenants are jurisdiction-sensitive and the team's posture matters.

### Attorneys' fees

Check for fee-shifting provisions and whether they are mutual, one-sided, or prevailing-party. Apply `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`.

### Backup and archival carveout

Check whether the destruction/return clause includes an exception for standard backup and archival retention systems. Apply the team's position from `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` â€” some teams require this carveout and will push to add it; others accept an NDA without it. If the playbook doesn't address this, ask.

### Governing law

Per `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` `## Playbook` â†’ `Governing law and venue`.

## Counterparty context

**BigCo NDAs:** Fortune 500 counterparties generally won't negotiate NDAs. Calibrate: is the RED flag truly a deal-breaker, or is it "different from our form"? If the business relationship matters, the call is whether to accept their paper â€” escalate that decision, don't make it.

**Startup NDAs:** Will usually take our paper. If their NDA has issues, the fastest path is often "let's use ours" rather than redlining theirs.

## Integration: CLM

If connected:
- GREEN â†’ offer to create the CLM record in the standard NDA workflow
- YELLOW â†’ offer to create it with a note attached listing the flagged items
- RED â†’ do not create a record; the lawyer decides what happens next

## What this skill does NOT do

- It does not negotiate. It sorts.
- It does not draft an NDA. If the answer is "use our paper," the user pulls our form from [CLM or document system].
- It does not make the call on YELLOW items. It surfaces them for a human.
- It does not state a position on any NDA term. Positions live in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`.

## Closing action

Read `~/.config/oscar/profile.json` â†’ `## NDA triage preferences` â†’ `closing_action`.

If configured, append the closing action verbatim at the end of every
output. Example configurations:

```
closing_action: "Send the full text of this analysis along with a copy
of the NDA to Legal at legal@[yourcompany].com for final confirmation before
signing."

closing_action: "Submit to [CLM] using the standard NDA workflow.
Legal will confirm before routing for signature."

closing_action: "Forward this output and the NDA to your contracts
manager."
```

If `closing_action` is not configured in `~/.config/oscar/profile.json`, append:
"Route final NDA through your standard approval process."

The cold-start interview asks: "When someone finishes an NDA
triage, what do you want them to do with the output? I'll add that as
a standing instruction at the end of every review."

## Close with the next-steps decision tree

End with the next-steps decision tree per CLAUDE.md `## Outputs`. Customize the options to what this skill just produced â€” the five default branches (draft the X, escalate, get more facts, watch and wait, something else) are a starting point, not a lock-in. The tree is the output; the lawyer picks.

---

# MUTUAL CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT

## Parties and Purpose

## **DATED: ___ 2026**

## **PARTIES**

(1) Stanford Industrial Supply Co.

(2) ACME Tooling Solutions Limited (registered in England and Wales no. 04123876)

## **BACKGROUND**

The parties wish to discuss a potential business arrangement relating to ACME's potential supply of specialist cutting tools and abrasives to Stanford (the "Purpose"). In connection with those discussions each party may disclose Confidential Information to the other.

## 1. Definition of Confidential Information

"Confidential Information" means any information disclosed by one party ("Discloser") to the other ("Recipient") in connection with the Purpose that is marked confidential, identified as confidential at the time of disclosure, or that a reasonable person would understand to be confidential given its nature and the circumstances of disclosure.

## 2. Carve-outs

Confidential Information does not include information that (a) is or becomes publicly available without breach of this Agreement; (b) was already known to the Recipient without obligation of confidence; (c) is received from a third party without restriction; (d) is independently developed without reference to the Discloser's information; or (e) is required to be disclosed by law or court order, provided that the Recipient gives prompt notice and reasonable cooperation.

## 3. Obligations

The Recipient shall (a) use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (b) protect it with the same degree of care as it uses for its own confidential information, but not less than reasonable care; (c) limit access to those of its personnel and professional advisers who need to know for the Purpose, and who are bound by equivalent obligations of confidence.

## 4. Term

This Agreement commences on the date above and continues for two (2) years. Obligations of confidence in respect of Confidential Information disclosed during the term survive for a further three (3) years from the date of disclosure.

## 5. Residuals

Nothing in this Agreement restricts the Recipient's use of residual information retained in the unaided memory of its personnel after the term, provided no Confidential Information is intentionally retained.

## 6. No Licence

No licence or other right is granted under any intellectual property of the Discloser by virtue of this Agreement.

## 7. Return or Destruction

On request or termination, the Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Information in its possession (other than back-up copies retained in the ordinary course) and certify destruction on request.

## Governing Law and Jurisdiction

This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of that jurisdiction.

## Miscellaneous

No Party may assign without the other's prior written consent. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts including by electronic signature. The Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties in relation to its subject matter and supersedes any prior arrangement or representation.

<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>

            <div class="logo-container">

</svg>

            </div>

            <div class="nav-title" id="nav-title"></div>

            <div class="nav-actions">
                <button id="copy-btn" title="Copy Markdown">
                    <svg id="copy-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <rect x="9" y="9" width="13" height="13" rx="2" ry="2"></rect>
                        <path d="M5 15H4a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V4a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h9a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v1"></path>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="copy-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
                <button id="open-btn" title="Open in Native App">
                    <svg id="open-icon" width="18" height="18" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" 
                        stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round">
                        <path d="M18 13v6a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2V8a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h6"></path>
                        <polyline points="15 3 21 3 21 9"></polyline>
                        <line x1="10" y1="14" x2="21" y2="3"></line>
                    </svg>
                    <span id="open-text" style="display: none;"></span>
                </button>
            </div>
        </nav>

        <div class="content-wrapper" id="content-wrapper">
            <div id="app">
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 36px; width: 40%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 100%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 95%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 90%; margin-bottom: 24px;"></div>
                <div class="skeleton" style="height: 16px; width: 80%; margin-bottom: 12px;"></div>
            </div>
            <div class="fade-overlay" id="fade-overlay"></div>
        </div>

        <div class="toggle-container" id="toggle-container">
            <button id="toggle-btn">
                <span id="toggle-text">Show More</span>
                <svg id="toggle-icon" width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor"
                    stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" style="transform: rotate(0deg);">
                    <polyline points="6 9 12 15 18 9"></polyline>
                </svg>
            </button>
        </div>
    </div>

    <script>
        const appDiv = document.getElementById('app');
        const INIT_ID = 1;
        let isExpanded = false; // START COLLAPSED BY DEFAULT!

        let rawMarkdownText = "";
        let currentFilePath = null;
        const navTitle = document.getElementById('nav-title');
        const copyBtn = document.getElementById('copy-btn');
        const copyIcon = document.getElementById('copy-icon');
        const copyText = document.getElementById('copy-text');
        const openBtn = document.getElementById('open-btn');
        const openText = document.getElementById('open-text');

        function fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(text) {
            return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
                const textArea = document.createElement("textarea");
                textArea.value = text;
                textArea.style.top = "0";
                textArea.style.left = "0";
                textArea.style.position = "fixed";
                textArea.style.opacity = "0";

                document.body.appendChild(textArea);
                textArea.focus();
                textArea.select();

                try {
                    const successful = document.execCommand('copy');
                    if (successful) {
                        resolve();
                    } else {
                        reject(new Error("execCommand returned false"));
                    }
                } catch (err) {
                    reject(err);
                } finally {
                    document.body.removeChild(textArea);
                }
            });
        }

        copyBtn.addEventListener('click', async () => {
            try {
                if (navigator.clipboard && window.isSecureContext) {
                    try {
                        await navigator.clipboard.writeText(rawMarkdownText);
                    } catch (e) {
                        await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                    }
                } else {
                    await fallbackCopyTextToClipboard(rawMarkdownText);
                }

                const originalSvg = copyIcon.innerHTML;
                copyIcon.innerHTML = `<polyline points="20 6 9 17 4 12"></polyline>`;

                copyText.textContent = "Copied!";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.style.color = "#10b981";

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyIcon.innerHTML = originalSvg;
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.style.color = "";
                }, 2000);
            } catch (err) {
                console.error('Failed to copy text: ', err);
                copyText.textContent = "Failed";
                copyText.style.display = "inline";
                copyBtn.title = err.toString();

                setTimeout(() => {
                    copyText.textContent = "";
                    copyText.style.display = "none";
                    copyBtn.title = "Copy Markdown";
                }, 3000);
            }
        });

        openBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
            if (!currentFilePath) return;

            const reqId = Date.now();

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                id: reqId,
                method: "tools/call",
                params: {
                    name: "open_local_file",
                    arguments: {
                        file_path: currentFilePath
                    }
                }
            }, "*");

            openText.textContent = "Opening...";
            openText.style.display = "inline";
            
            setTimeout(() => {
                openText.style.display = "none";
                openText.textContent = "";
            }, 2000);
        });

        function escapeHtml(unsafe) {
            return (unsafe || "").toString()
                .replace(/&/g, "&")
                .replace(/</g, "<")
                .replace(/>/g, ">")
                .replace(/"/g, """)
                .replace(/'/g, "'");
        }

        function showError(title, message, details = null) {
            let html = `
            <div class="alert">
                <div class="alert-title">
                    <svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 24 24" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round"><circle cx="12" cy="12" r="10"></circle><line x1="12" y1="8" x2="12" y2="12"></line><line x1="12" y1="16" x2="12.01" y2="16"></line></svg>
                    ${escapeHtml(title)}
                </div>
                <div class="alert-body">${escapeHtml(message)}</div>
            `;
            if (details) {
                html += `<div class="alert-details">${escapeHtml(details)}</div>`;
            }
            html += `</div>`;
            appDiv.innerHTML = html;
        }

        function checkCollapsible() {
            const wrapper = document.getElementById('content-wrapper');
            const toggleContainer = document.getElementById('toggle-container');
            const fadeOverlay = document.getElementById('fade-overlay');
            const toggleIcon = document.getElementById('toggle-icon');
            const toggleText = document.getElementById('toggle-text');

            // 1. If no markdown, we are showing the skeleton. Do not collapse!
            if (!rawMarkdownText) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                triggerResize();
                return;
            }

            // 2. Data is loaded
            const realHeight = appDiv.scrollHeight;
            const COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD = 300;
            const COLLAPSED_HEIGHT = 150;
            const EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT = 500;

            if (realHeight > COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD) {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'flex';
                if (isExpanded) {
                    // Open view! Scroll inside max 500px window
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = EXPANDED_MAX_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'auto';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(180deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show Less';
                } else {
                    // Collapsed View
                    wrapper.style.maxHeight = COLLAPSED_HEIGHT + 'px';
                    wrapper.style.overflowY = 'hidden';
                    fadeOverlay.style.display = 'block';
                    toggleIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(0deg)';
                    toggleText.textContent = 'Show More';
                }
            } else {
                toggleContainer.style.display = 'none';
                wrapper.style.maxHeight = 'none';
                wrapper.style.overflowY = 'visible';
                fadeOverlay.style.display = 'none';
            }

            triggerResize();
        }

        document.getElementById('toggle-btn').addEventListener('click', () => {
            isExpanded = !isExpanded;
            checkCollapsible();
        });

        function triggerResize() {
            // Measure the exact unconstrained height of the outer card
            const card = document.querySelector('.adeu-card');
            const containerChrome = 34; // 16px padding top/bottom + 2px borders

            const requestedHeight = card.offsetHeight + containerChrome;

            window.parent.postMessage({
                jsonrpc: "2.0",
                method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                params: { height: Math.ceil(requestedHeight), width: 600 }
            }, "*");
        }

        window.addEventListener("message", (event) => {
            try {
                if (!event.data || event.data.jsonrpc !== "2.0") return;
                const msg = event.data;

                if (msg.id === INIT_ID) {
                    window.parent.postMessage({
                        jsonrpc: "2.0",
                        method: "ui/notifications/initialized",
                        params: {}
                    }, "*");
                    return;
                }

                if (msg.method === "ui/notifications/tool-result") {
                    if (window.__MARKED_ERROR || typeof marked === 'undefined') {
                        showError("Missing Dependency", "The Markdown renderer failed to load.", window.__MARKED_ERROR || "marked is undefined.");
                        return;
                    }

                    const result = msg.params;

                    // If the state is pending, or no markdown is returned, completely hide the UI
                    if (!result || !result.structuredContent || result.structuredContent.status === "pending" || !result.structuredContent.markdown) {
                        document.documentElement.style.display = 'none';
                        window.parent.postMessage({
                            jsonrpc: "2.0",
                            method: "ui/notifications/size-changed",
                            params: { height: 0, width: 0 }
                        }, "*");
                        return;
                    }

                    // Render Markdown
                    rawMarkdownText = result.structuredContent.markdown;
                    if (result.structuredContent.file_path) {
                        currentFilePath = result.structuredContent.file_path;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'flex';
                    } else {
                        currentFilePath = null;
                        openBtn.style.display = 'none';
                    }
                    appDiv.innerHTML = marked.parse(rawMarkdownText);
                    copyBtn.style.display = 'flex';

                    if (result.structuredContent.title) {
                        navTitle.textContent = result.structuredContent.title;
                        navTitle.title = result.structuredContent.title;
                    }
                }
            } catch (error) {
                showError("Rendering Engine Error", "An unexpected error occurred.", error.toString() + "\n" + error.stack);
            }
        });

        const observer = new ResizeObserver(() => {
            checkCollapsible();
        });
        observer.observe(appDiv);

        window.parent.postMessage({
            jsonrpc: "2.0",
            id: INIT_ID,
            method: "ui/initialize",
            params: {
                appInfo: { name: "Adeu Markdown UI", version: "1.1.0" },
                appCapabilities: {},
                protocolVersion: "2025-11-21"
            }
        }, "*");
    </script>
</body>

</html>

> **File Path:** `/root/Documents/Oscar GC/Commercial/S32-807215 Sprint 30 â€” 10-NDA triage c16/nda-betacorp-logistics.docx`

# NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

## **DATED: ___ 2026**

## **PARTIES**

(1) BetaCorp Logistics PLC ("Discloser")

(2) Stanford Industrial Supply Co. ("Recipient")

## **BACKGROUND**

The parties wish to discuss a potential business arrangement relating to BetaCorp's evaluation of Stanford as a potential supplier of MRO consumables to its UK distribution network (the "Purpose"). In connection with those discussions each party may disclose Confidential Information to the other.

## 1. Definitions

"Confidential Information" means all information disclosed by Discloser to Recipient, in any form, in connection with the Purpose, whether or not marked or identified as confidential.

## 2. Obligations

Recipient shall (a) use Confidential Information solely for the Purpose; (b) protect it with the same degree of care it uses for its own most sensitive information; (c) restrict access to those personnel and advisers who have a need to know.

## 3. Carve-outs

Confidential Information does not include information that is or becomes publicly available without breach, was already known to Recipient without obligation of confidence, is received from a third party without restriction, or is independently developed.

## 4. Term

This Agreement commences on the date above and continues for five (5) years. The Recipient's obligations of confidence survive for a further seven (7) years after termination or expiry.

## 5. No Disclosure of Discussions

Recipient shall not disclose to any third party the existence or substance of any discussions between the parties without Discloser's prior written consent.

## 6. Return

On request, Recipient shall return or destroy all Confidential Information at Discloser's election. The Recipient shall certify destruction within five (5) Business Days.

## 7. Remedies

Recipient acknowledges that damages may not be an adequate remedy for breach and that Discloser is entitled to seek injunctive relief.

## Governing Law and Jurisdiction

This Agreement is governed by the laws of England and Wales and the parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of that jurisdiction.

## Miscellaneous

No Party may assign without the other's prior written consent. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts including by electronic signature. The Agreement contains the entire understanding between the parties in relation to its subject matter and supersedes any prior arrangement or representation.

contents.text:
<!-- FILE: src/adeu/templates/markdown_ui.html -->
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>

<head>
    <meta charset="utf-8">
    <title>Adeu Markdown UI</title>
    <meta name="color-scheme" content="light dark">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
    <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
    <link
        href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Jost:wght@400;500;600&family=Lora:ital,wght@0,600;1,600&display=swap"
        rel="stylesheet">
    <style>
        :root {
            --brand-sand: #e8c0a1;
            --brand-sand-light: #f2d9c7;
            --brand-sand-dark: #d4a882;
            --brand-mint: #e6f3f0;
            --brand-mint-dark: #c9e4de;
            --brand-navy: #1a2a2c;
            --brand-navy-light: #2a3a3c;
            --brand-mauve: #dacad2;
            --neutral-50: #fafafa;
            --neutral-100: #f5f5f5;
            --neutral-200: #e5e5e5;
            --neutral-300: #d9d9d9;
            --neutral-600: #555969;
            --neutral-800: #1e2028;
            --neutral-900: #171717;
            --font-lora: 'Lora', Georgia, serif;
            --font-jost: 'Jost', system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, sans-serif;
            --bg-body: var(--neutral-50);
            --bg-card: #ffffff;
            --border-card: var(--neutral-200);
            --text-main: var(--neutral-600);
            --text-heading: var(--brand-navy);
            --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.15);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            :root {
                --bg-body: var(--neutral-900);
                --bg-card: var(--brand-navy);
                --border-card: var(--brand-navy-light);
                --text-main: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.85);
                --text-heading: #ffffff;
                --bg-blockquote: rgba(232, 192, 161, 0.1);
                --neutral-100: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        html,
        body {
            margin: 0;
            padding: 0;
            box-sizing: border-box;
            background-color: transparent;
        }

        body {
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            color: var(--text-main);
            line-height: 1.6;
            -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased;
            padding: 16px;
        }

        .adeu-card {
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            border: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 12px;
            overflow: hidden;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
        }

        .navbar {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
            padding: 16px 32px;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: space-between;
            position: relative;
        }

        .logo-container {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            flex: 1;
        }

        .nav-title {
            position: absolute;
            left: 50%;
            transform: translateX(-50%);
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-size: 1.1rem;
            white-space: nowrap;
            overflow: hidden;
            text-overflow: ellipsis;
            max-width: 40%;
            text-align: center;
        }

        .nav-actions {
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            justify-content: flex-end;
            flex: 1;
        }

        #copy-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #copy-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        #open-btn {
            display: none;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--text-main);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-size: 0.85rem;
            font-weight: 500;
            cursor: pointer;
            padding: 8px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #open-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #copy-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
            #open-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        .logo-container svg {
            max-width: 80px;
            height: auto;
        }

        .logo-container svg path,
        .logo-container svg rect,
        .logo-container svg polygon,
        .logo-container svg circle {
            transition: fill 0.2s ease;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {

            .logo-container svg path,
            .logo-container svg rect,
            .logo-container svg polygon,
            .logo-container svg circle,
            .logo-container svg {
                fill: #ffffff !important;
            }
        }

        .content-wrapper {
            position: relative;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar {
            width: 6px;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-track {
            background: transparent;
        }

        .content-wrapper::-webkit-scrollbar-thumb {
            background-color: var(--border-card);
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        #app {
            padding: 32px;
            display: flow-root;
        }

        .fade-overlay {
            position: absolute;
            bottom: 0;
            left: 0;
            right: 0;
            height: 80px;
            background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(255, 255, 255, 0), var(--bg-card));
            pointer-events: none;
            display: none;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .fade-overlay {
                background: linear-gradient(to bottom, rgba(26, 42, 44, 0), var(--bg-card));
            }
        }

        .toggle-container {
            flex-shrink: 0;
            display: none;
            justify-content: center;
            align-items: center;
            padding: 12px;
            border-top: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            background-color: var(--bg-card);
        }

        #toggle-btn {
            background: transparent;
            border: none;
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            font-family: var(--font-jost);
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 0.95rem;
            cursor: pointer;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 6px;
            padding: 8px 16px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            transition: all 0.2s ease;
        }

        #toggle-btn:hover {
            color: var(--text-heading);
            background: var(--neutral-100);
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            #toggle-btn:hover {
                background: var(--brand-navy-light);
            }
        }

        #toggle-icon {
            transition: transform 0.3s ease;
        }

        h1,
        h2,
        h3,
        h4 {
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
            color: var(--text-heading);
            font-weight: 600;
            letter-spacing: -0.02em;
            margin-top: 1.5em;
            margin-bottom: 0.5em;
        }

        h1 {
            font-size: 2.25rem;
            border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border-card);
            padding-bottom: 0.3em;
            margin-top: 0;
        }

        h2 {
            font-size: 1.75rem;
        }

        h3 {
            font-size: 1.25rem;
        }

        a {
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            text-decoration: none;
            border-bottom: 1px solid transparent;
            transition: border 0.2s ease;
        }

        a:hover {
            border-bottom-color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
        }

        strong {
            font-weight: 600;
            color: var(--text-heading);
        }

        ul,
        ol {
            padding-left: 1.5rem;
            margin-bottom: 1rem;
        }

        li {
            margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
        }

        blockquote {
            border-left: 4px solid var(--brand-sand);
            padding: 12px 20px;
            margin: 24px 0;
            color: var(--text-main);
            background: var(--bg-blockquote);
            border-radius: 0 8px 8px 0;
            font-style: italic;
        }

        code {
            font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, "Liberation Mono", Menlo, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            background: var(--neutral-100);
            color: var(--brand-sand-dark);
            padding: 0.2em 0.4em;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        pre code {
            display: block;
            padding: 16px;
            background: var(--neutral-900);
            color: #e5e5e5;
            border-radius: 8px;
            overflow-x: auto;
        }

        .skeleton {
            background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--neutral-100) 25%, var(--border-card) 50%, var(--neutral-100) 75%);
            background-size: 200% 100%;
            animation: skeleton 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
            border-radius: 4px;
        }

        @keyframes skeleton {
            0% {
                background-position: 200% 0;
            }

            100% {
                background-position: -200% 0;
            }
        }

        .alert {
            padding: 16px;
            border-radius: 8px;
            margin-bottom: 20px;
            display: flex;
            flex-direction: column;
            gap: 8px;
            background-color: #fef2f2;
            border: 1px solid #fecaca;
            color: #991b1b;
        }

        @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
            .alert {
                background-color: rgba(127, 29, 29, 0.2);
                border-color: rgba(248, 113, 113, 0.3);
                color: #fca5a5;
            }
        }

        .alert-title {
            font-weight: 600;
            font-size: 1.1em;
            margin: 0;
            display: flex;
            align-items: center;
            gap: 8px;
            font-family: var(--font-lora);
        }

        .alert-body {
            margin: 0;
            font-size: 0.95em;
        }

        .alert-details {
            background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.05);
            padding: 12px;
            border-radius: 6px;
            font-family: ui-monospace, Consolas, monospace;
            font-size: 0.85em;
            white-space: pre-wrap;
            word-break: break-all;
            margin-top: 8px;
            border: 1px solid rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.1);
        }
    </style>
